update_summary: Added Wrona et al. 2025 (ACM) as new partial_overlap; added Zhu 2026 (AAAI/arXiv:2506.12492) as related_work for H2; confirmed no new collisions on H3/H4/H5b/H6 (all new hypothesis elements remain novel); overall novelty unchanged at HIGH

candidates.title: Order Matters: Empirical and Theory-Grounded Study of Augmentation Operation Sequencing in CNNs and ViTs

collisions.paper: Wrona, Atanasious, Delli Priscoli. 2025. Deep Reinforcement Learning-based Automatic Augmentation for Gastrointestinal Disease Classification. ACM Transactions on Computing for Healthcare. DOI:10.1145/3726875.
collisions.overlap: Performs brute-force permutation search (7P2 = 42 two-step orderings) to find best augmentation operation sequence for gastrointestinal disease classification. Explicitly states 'sequence matters because geometric and color operations are non-linear.' Finds sharpness-first ordering outperforms others.
collisions.differentiation: Wrona et al. treat ordering search as a preliminary offline step for a DRL augmentation controller — ordering sensitivity is not the central research question. Key differences: (a) only 2-step orderings (7P2), not all K! permutations of a fixed K-op set; (b) medical images (gastrointestinal), not natural images (CIFAR-10/100); (c) Nature CNN only, no cross-architecture comparison (CNN vs. ViT); (d) no statistical rigor (no paired-seed design, no paired t-test, no Bonferroni correction); (e) no theoretical framework (no NC measure, no DPI principle, no generalization bound); (f) no category-level interleaving or magnitude interaction study. Must cite and explicitly differentiate in related work.

collisions.paper: Li et al., 2024. Learning Tree-Structured Composition of Data Augmentation. arXiv:2408.14381
collisions.overlap: Studies augmentation composition structure (binary-tree vs. sequential) via search algorithms. Involves ordered augmentation sequences in the search space.
collisions.differentiation: Li et al. optimize composition structure as a search problem for efficiency. They do not isolate ordering as the independent variable, do not compare fixed permutations of identical K-operation sets, provide no theoretical framework predicting why ordering should matter, and do not study architecture sensitivity. Research question is fundamentally different: search efficiency vs. ordering sensitivity study.

collisions.paper: Cubuk et al., 2018. AutoAugment: Learning Augmentation Policies from Data. arXiv:1805.09501
collisions.overlap: Searches over ordered pairs of operations; policies implicitly encode ordering.
collisions.differentiation: AutoAugment optimizes (operation, probability, magnitude) without ablating order. Never tests whether permuting operations in a found policy changes accuracy.

collisions.overlap: Applies N random operations per image in random order per image.
collisions.differentiation: Random ordering is a design choice to avoid specifying order, not a controlled comparison of fixed permutations.

collisions.paper: Ho et al., 2019. Population Based Augmentation. arXiv:1905.05393
collisions.overlap: Shows that shuffling the epoch-level augmentation schedule degrades accuracy.
collisions.differentiation: PBA operates at epoch-level curriculum granularity (which policy to apply during which training epoch), not within-image operation ordering granularity.

collisions.paper: Ratner et al., 2017. TANDA: Learning to Compose Domain-Specific Transformations. arXiv:1709.01643
collisions.overlap: Uses LSTM to learn full augmentation sequences, implicitly learning something about ordering.
collisions.differentiation: TANDA learns which operations to include and their sequence simultaneously. No ordering ablation within a fixed operation set.

collisions.paper: Zhu, 2026. Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning Strategies for Hypertensive Retinopathy Detection. arXiv:2506.12492 (AAAI 2026).
collisions.overlap: Studies architecture-dependent augmentation effects: pure ViTs benefit from augmentation (Kappa 0.250→0.472 for ViT-B/8) while hybrid ViT-CNN models suffer (CAFormer Kappa 0.472→0.333). Relates to H2 mechanistic claim about ViT inductive bias and augmentation sensitivity.
collisions.differentiation: Zhu 2026 studies augmentation presence vs. absence, not operation ordering/permutation. The independent variable is augmentation presence and choice, not sequence order. No ordering ablation, no permutation comparison, no theory, medical images domain. Actually provides supporting motivation for H2: ViTs' weaker inductive bias makes them more sensitive to augmentation design choices.

candidates.differentiation_notes: The central contribution — first controlled study isolating ordering as the sole independent variable with theory-grounded framework (feature-space NC_2 + corrected DPI), cross-architecture comparison (CNN vs. ViT), category-level interleaved ordering, magnitude interaction, and proper statistical inference — is confirmed novel. New element H3 (feature-space NC_2, rho>0.5 threshold, 10k samples in 512-D), H5b (interleaved P→G ordering), and H6 (inverted-U magnitude) have no direct prior art. Wrona et al. 2025 is the new closest empirical neighbor and must be proactively differentiated in related work.
new_prior_art_since_last_report: Wrona et al. 2025 (ACM 10.1145/3726875) — partial_overlap: brute-force permutation search for 2-step orderings in medical images, no theory, no architecture comparison
Zhu 2026 (arXiv:2506.12492) — related_work: architecture-dependent augmentation effects (presence, not ordering)

candidates.title: Variance Decomposition of Augmentation Pipeline Design Choices

collisions.paper: Liu & Mirzasoleiman, 2022. Data-Efficient Augmentation for Training Neural Networks. arXiv:2210.08363
collisions.overlap: Studies Jacobian-level theory of augmentation effects on generalization; provides augmentation subset selection theory.
collisions.differentiation: Does not decompose variance across ordering/selection/magnitude/seed dimensions. Different theoretical and empirical framework.

collisions.overlap: Demonstrates that magnitude (M) is a key driver of performance.
collisions.differentiation: RandAugment studies magnitude as its main knob but does not perform formal variance decomposition across design dimensions.

candidates.differentiation_notes: Variance decomposition framing is novel. Activation condition: pilot shows spread < 0.2% after full training. Unlikely given strong pilot evidence. No new competitors found.

candidates.title: Class-Level Effects of Augmentation Ordering

collisions.paper: Kirichenko et al., 2023. Understanding the Detrimental Class-Level Effects of Data Augmentation. NeurIPS 2023.
collisions.overlap: Studies how augmentation strength affects per-class accuracy differently.
collisions.differentiation: Kirichenko et al. vary augmentation strength (a scalar), not ordering. Different independent variable. Must cite with explicit differentiation.

candidates.differentiation_notes: Best positioned as secondary analysis within cand_a paper (zero additional training cost). No new competitors found.

key_findings: No prior paper isolates augmentation operation ordering (permutation within a fixed-length per-image pipeline) as the central research question for natural image classification.

NEW: Wrona et al. 2025 (ACM) performs brute-force 2-step permutation search for medical images — partial overlap but treats ordering as preprocessing detail, not central question; no theory, no cross-architecture comparison.
NEW: Zhu 2026 (AAAI) shows architecture-dependent augmentation effects (ViT benefits, hybrid suffers) — related_work supporting H2 motivation, but studies presence/absence not ordering.
The Wasserstein Non-Commutativity (NC_2) measure — in feature space — has no prior art.
The corrected DPI reversibility principle (Flip-first as optimal, eta=1 for bijective transforms) has no prior art.
The category-level interleaved ordering study (P→G→P→G vs. block ordering) has no prior art.
The inverted-U magnitude interaction (H6: M9 > M5, M14) has no prior art.
Two survey papers (Cheung & Yeung 2023, Yang et al. 2023) explicitly identify per-image operation ordering as an open, unaddressed question.
recommended_related_work_citations: Wrona et al. (2025). ACM Trans. Computing for Healthcare, DOI:10.1145/3726875 — NEW: brute-force permutation search for 2-step orderings (differentiate carefully: medical domain, no theory, no architecture comparison)
Li et al. (2024). arXiv:2408.14381 — composition structure search (contrast carefully)
Ho et al. (2019). arXiv:1905.05393 — PBA (epoch-level ordering)
Zhu (2026). arXiv:2506.12492 — NEW: architecture-dependent augmentation effects (cite as motivation for H2)
Kirichenko et al. (NeurIPS 2023) — class-level augmentation strength effects
Liu & Mirzasoleiman (2022). arXiv:2210.08363 — data-efficient augmentation theory
Cheung & Yeung (IEEE TNNLS 2023) — survey identifying ordering as open question
Yang et al. (KAIS 2023) — survey identifying ordering as open question
